items.title: Italian artist uses flares to explore robot-human relationship
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>On the United Nations International Day of Light on May 16, the town of Brixen in the Italian Alps was lit up by a series of SOS flares, a symbolic global call to attention concerning the future relationship between robots and humans. The flares were ignited by Italian artist Stefano Cagol as part of an artistic performance titled "We Own the Futures.”

#Artist #StefanoCagol #Art #UnitedNations #DayofLight #Reuters #News

items.title: Starship's next test and Musk's space vision
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Elon Musk has stepped away from DOGE. His ninth Starship test flight could launch as early as next week, with a speech by Musk laying out his priorities for SpaceX. Space correspondent Joey Roulette joins the podcast to explore Musk's celestial ambitions and what SpaceX means to the world - and beyond.

items.title: ‘Phantom' cows, missing millions spark scandal in Uruguay
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Sandra Palleiro is on the hunt for her lost cows. The 60-year-old accountant is standing in a muddy field at the end of a farm track in Uruguay's remote border region with Brazil.

She has traveled 370 miles from the capital, Montevideo, to find 61 cattle she owns, at least on paper. The missing bovines were part of a 'cow bond' scheme that has collapsed, causing one of Uruguay's biggest ever financial scandals.

Politicians, radio hosts, pensioners and even priests are among the thousands now attempting to recover their savings — and their missing 'phantom' cattle, the number of which is proving hard to pin down. 

#PhantomCows #CowBonds #Uruguay #missingCows #cows #cattle #bonds #scandalissingCows #cows #cattle #bonds #scandal #News #Reuters #Newsfeed
